title: Frontend clarity pass for first successful dictation
summary: Frontend-only implementation improved first-successful-dictation clarity by adding readiness messaging in auth, a clearer first-launch hint, and a small first-run onboarding sentence.
lessons: Night Shift correctly prioritized first successful dictation.
Codex implemented a safe frontend-only clarity improvement.
Future UI clarity tasks should prefer frontend-only scope before backend/auth/Rust changes.
Night Shift initially proposed too many sensitive files; future task generation should narrow scope.

title: Fix: First successful dictation — activation_failed retry state
summary: V6 handoff loop generated a scoped implementation task from an approved product proposal. Implementation added a visible fallback error message and an active retry button for the activation_failed state. Out-of-scope formatting changes were cleaned before commit. All hooks passed: Prettier, ESLint, translation check.
tests_reported: manual test pending — all 5 activation states
lessons: V6 handoff successfully converted a product patch proposal into a scoped implementation task.
Product implementation must be checked for formatting-only out-of-scope changes before commit.
For activation UI fixes, frontend-only AuthPortal.tsx changes can be enough when useAuthFlow already exposes the required callback/state.
Handoff scope rules worked: no backend, no auth client, no license client, no Rust.

title: V12 Experiment 1 — Windows paste restore delay floor 450ms → 150ms
summary: Reduced Windows clipboard restore delay floor from 450ms to 150ms (clipboard.rs:120). Founder smoke tests passed: Notepad, VS Code, Chrome/Gmail — no paste failure, no clipboard restore failure. Full 21-case matrix (Slack, Teams, Word still pending). No post-fix paste_latency_ms benchmarks recorded yet.
tests_reported: formal paste_latency_ms benchmark: pending (≥5 observations needed)
lessons: 150ms restore floor passed native and browser apps on first smoke test.
Electron apps (Slack, Teams) require dedicated testing — not yet confirmed.
V12 improve loop (propose → gate → implement → test → measure → compare → learn) executed successfully for Phase 1–4.
One-line Rust change requires cargo check + manual paste validation, not just compile — OS timing behaviour cannot be unit-tested.

title: Idle background inference loop — diagnosis + settings confirmation
summary: Read-only investigation of audio.rs, transcription/inference.rs, wake_word.rs, transcribe.rs, recorder.rs. Confirmed two root causes: RC-1 (wake-word silence-gate missing) and RC-2 (stuck recording session). Local settings inspection confirmed wake_word_enabled=false, always_on_microphone=true, model_unload_timeout=never. RC-1 not active on this machine. RC-2 (stuck recording session in chunking worker) is primary cause. Recommended: investigate stop_transcription_action binding_id mismatch + defensive sampler timeout before patching.
lessons: wake_word_enabled must be checked before recommending a silence-gate patch
always_on_microphone=true + VAD hangover is enough to cause ambient chunk accumulation in a stuck recording session
model_unload_timeout=never means model RAM is by design — not a bug for this machine
Stuck recording sessions are the primary RAM growth driver; investigate stop path before implementing defensive timeout

title: RC-2 Patch 1 — stuck recording diagnostic logs
summary: Logging-only instrumentation for stuck recording session diagnosis. Promotes stop_transcription_action entry from debug to info. Promotes binding_id mismatch guard from debug to warn — makes silent stop drops visible. Adds sampler-thread elapsed/chunk counters: warns after 5 min of running (then every 60 s), logs exit with elapsed time and chunk count. No behaviour change.
tests_reported: cargo check — Finished dev profile 11.10s, 0 errors
next_action: run Vocalype until stuck-session issue reproduces, then inspect new warn logs to confirm whether Path 2B (binding_id mismatch) or Path 2A (no stop sent) is the true cause

title: V12 Smoke Test Complete — all available apps passed (21/21 cases)
summary: V12 paste delay patch (450ms -> 150ms floor, clipboard.rs:120) smoke tested across all available apps. Notepad, VS Code, Chrome, Gmail, Slack, Teams, Word all passed T1/T2/T3 (3 cases each, 21 total). No paste failure observed. No clipboard restore failure observed. Decision: PROVISIONAL_KEEP maintained — patch confirmed stable on all tested targets. Remaining requirement: >=5 post-fix paste_latency_ms observations to confirm median < 420ms and upgrade to FULL_KEEP.
lessons: 150ms restore floor is safe across native apps (Notepad, Word), browser apps (Chrome, Gmail), Electron apps (VS Code, Slack, Teams), and Office apps (Word).
Full 21-case smoke test matrix passed — no regression in any tested app category.
PROVISIONAL_KEEP is maintained until >=5 post-fix paste_latency_ms observations confirm median < 420ms.
Upgrade to FULL_KEEP: record observations with add_benchmark_observation.py --notes 'post-fix floor=150ms', then re-run the agent.
